questions.question: Which Gestalt principle explains why you perceive a circle even when part of its outline is missing?
questions.explanation: The Gestalt principle of closure describes the brain's tendency to fill in gaps in incomplete figures to perceive them as complete, whole shapes. Even when part of a circle's outline is missing, we perceive it as a full circle because our brain fills in the gaps.

questions.question: Binocular disparity is a depth cue that relies on which of the following?

options.text: The fact that distant objects appear smaller than nearby objects
The slightly different images received by each eye due to their horizontal separation
The convergence of parallel lines at the horizon
Changes in object texture as distance increases

questions.explanation: Binocular disparity occurs because our two eyes are separated horizontally by a few inches, so each eye receives a slightly different image. The brain fuses these two images and uses the degree of difference to calculate depth, with closer objects producing greater disparity.
